problems.title: Reverse Linked List
problems.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list's head.

solutions.python:
def reverse_list(head):
    prev = None
    curr = head
    while curr:
        nxt = curr.next
        curr.next = prev
        prev = curr
        curr = nxt
    return prev

problems.title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal

problems.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array

problems.title: Subsets
problems.description: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set).

solutions.python:
def subsets(nums):
    result = []
    path = []
    def backtrack(start):
        result.append(path[:])
        for i in range(start, len(nums)):
            path.append(nums[i])
            backtrack(i + 1)
            path.pop()
    backtrack(0)
    return result

problems.title: Product of Array Except Self
problems.description: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all elements of nums except nums[i], without using division, in O(n) time.

solutions.python:
def product_except_self(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    result = [1] * n
    prefix = 1
    for i in range(n):
        result[i] = prefix
        prefix *= nums[i]
    suffix = 1
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        result[i] *= suffix
        suffix *= nums[i]
    return result

problems.title: 3Sum
problems.description: Given an integer array nums, return all unique triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j != k and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0.

problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problems.description: Given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a stock on day i, maximize profit by choosing a single day to buy and a later day to sell. Return the max profit, or 0 if none possible.

solutions.python:
def max_profit(prices):
    min_price = float('inf')
    best = 0
    for p in prices:
        min_price = min(min_price, p)
        best = max(best, p - min_price)
    return best

problems.title: Insert Interval
problems.description: Given a set of non-overlapping intervals sorted by start, insert a new interval into the correct position, merging as necessary.

solutions.python:
def insert(intervals, new_interval):
    result = []
    i = 0
    n = len(intervals)
    while i < n and intervals[i][1] < new_interval[0]:
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1
    while i < n and intervals[i][0] <= new_interval[1]:
        new_interval[0] = min(new_interval[0], intervals[i][0])
        new_interval[1] = max(new_interval[1], intervals[i][1])
        i += 1
    result.append(new_interval)
    while i < n:
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1
    return result

problems.title: Add Two Numbers
problems.description: Given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers stored in reverse order (one digit per node), add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list.

solutions.python:
def add_two_numbers(l1, l2):
    dummy = ListNode()
    curr = dummy
    carry = 0
    while l1 or l2 or carry:
        v1 = l1.val if l1 else 0
        v2 = l2.val if l2 else 0
        total = v1 + v2 + carry
        carry = total // 10
        curr.next = ListNode(total % 10)
        curr = curr.next
        l1 = l1.next if l1 else None
        l2 = l2.next if l2 else None
    return dummy.next

problems.title: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements, in better than O(n log n) time where n is the array size.

solutions.python:
import heapq
from collections import Counter
def top_k_frequent(nums, k):
    counts = Counter(nums)
    return [num for num, freq in heapq.nlargest(k, counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])]

problems.title: N-Queens
problems.description: Place n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other, and return all distinct solutions (each as a board configuration).

solutions.python:
def solve_n_queens(n):
    result = []
    cols = set()
    diag1 = set()
    diag2 = set()
    board = [['.'] * n for _ in range(n)]
    def backtrack(row):
        if row == n:
            result.append([''.join(r) for r in board])
            return
        for col in range(n):
            if col in cols or (row - col) in diag1 or (row + col) in diag2:
                continue
            cols.add(col); diag1.add(row - col); diag2.add(row + col)
            board[row][col] = 'Q'
            backtrack(row + 1)
            board[row][col] = '.'
            cols.remove(col); diag1.remove(row - col); diag2.remove(row + col)
    backtrack(0)
    return result

problems.title: Subsets II
problems.description: Given an integer array nums that may contain duplicates, return all possible subsets (the power set) without duplicate subsets.

solutions.python:
def subsets_with_dup(nums):
    nums.sort()
    result = []
    path = []
    def backtrack(start):
        result.append(path[:])
        for i in range(start, len(nums)):
            if i > start and nums[i] == nums[i - 1]:
                continue
            path.append(nums[i])
            backtrack(i + 1)
            path.pop()
    backtrack(0)
    return result

problems.title: Gas Station
problems.description: There are n gas stations along a circular route with gas[i] fuel and cost[i] to travel to the next station. Return the starting station index to complete the circuit, or -1 if impossible (guaranteed unique solution if one exists).

solutions.python:
def can_complete_circuit(gas, cost):
    total = 0
    tank = 0
    start = 0
    for i in range(len(gas)):
        diff = gas[i] - cost[i]
        total += diff
        tank += diff
        if tank < 0:
            start = i + 1
            tank = 0
    return start if total >= 0 else -1

problems.title: Multiply Strings
problems.description: Given two non-negative integers num1 and num2 represented as strings, return the product of num1 and num2, also represented as a string, without using built-in big-integer conversion or direct arithmetic on the whole numbers.

solutions.python:
def multiply(num1, num2):
    if num1 == '0' or num2 == '0':
        return '0'
    m, n = len(num1), len(num2)
    result = [0] * (m + n)
    for i in range(m - 1, -1, -1):
        for j in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            mul = int(num1[i]) * int(num2[j])
            p1, p2 = i + j, i + j + 1
            total = mul + result[p2]
            result[p2] = total % 10
            result[p1] += total // 10
    result_str = ''.join(map(str, result)).lstrip('0')
    return result_str if result_str else '0'

problems.title: Odd Even Linked List
problems.description: Given a singly linked list, group all nodes at odd indices together followed by nodes at even indices, and return the reordered list, in O(1) extra space.

solutions.python:
def odd_even_list(head):
    if not head or not head.next:
        return head
    odd = head
    even = head.next
    even_head = even
    while even and even.next:
        odd.next = even.next
        odd = odd.next
        even.next = odd.next
        even = even.next
    odd.next = even_head
    return head

problems.title: Palindrome Linked List
problems.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return true if it is a palindrome, using O(1) extra space.

solutions.python:
def is_palindrome(head):
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    prev = None
    while slow:
        nxt = slow.next
        slow.next = prev
        prev = slow
        slow = nxt
    left, right = head, prev
    while right:
        if left.val != right.val:
            return False
        left = left.next
        right = right.next
    return True

problems.title: IPO
problems.description: Given available capital w, a max number of projects k you can pick, and arrays of profits and required capital per project, return the maximum capital you can have after finishing at most k projects.

solutions.python:
import heapq
def find_maximized_capital(k, w, profits, capital):
    projects = sorted(zip(capital, profits))
    max_heap = []
    i = 0
    n = len(projects)
    for _ in range(k):
        while i < n and projects[i][0] <= w:
            heapq.heappush(max_heap, -projects[i][1])
            i += 1
        if not max_heap:
            break
        w += -heapq.heappop(max_heap)
    return w

problems.title: Reverse Linked List II
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list and integers left and right, reverse the nodes from position left to position right (1-indexed), and return the modified list.

solutions.python:
def reverse_between(head, left, right):
    dummy = ListNode(0, head)
    prev = dummy
    for _ in range(left - 1):
        prev = prev.next
    curr = prev.next
    for _ in range(right - left):
        nxt = curr.next
        curr.next = nxt.next
        nxt.next = prev.next
        prev.next = nxt
    return dummy.next

problems.title: Rotate List
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list, rotate the list to the right by k places.

solutions.python:
def rotate_right(head, k):
    if not head or not head.next:
        return head
    length = 1
    tail = head
    while tail.next:
        tail = tail.next
        length += 1
    k %= length
    if k == 0:
        return head
    tail.next = head
    steps_to_new_tail = length - k
    new_tail = head
    for _ in range(steps_to_new_tail - 1):
        new_tail = new_tail.next
    new_head = new_tail.next
    new_tail.next = None
    return new_head

problems.title: Binary Tree Inorder Traversal
problems.description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the inorder traversal of its nodes' values, using an iterative approach with an explicit stack.

solutions.python:
def inorder_traversal(root):
    result = []
    stack = []
    node = root
    while stack or node:
        while node:
            stack.append(node)
            node = node.left
        node = stack.pop()
        result.append(node.val)
        node = node.right
    return result

problems.title: Reorganize String
problems.description: Given a string s, rearrange its characters so no two adjacent characters are the same, and return any valid rearrangement, or an empty string if impossible.

solutions.python:
import heapq
from collections import Counter
def reorganize_string(s):
    counts = Counter(s)
    heap = [(-freq, c) for c, freq in counts.items()]
    heapq.heapify(heap)
    result = []
    prev_freq, prev_char = 0, ''
    while heap:
        freq, c = heapq.heappop(heap)
        result.append(c)
        if prev_freq < 0:
            heapq.heappush(heap, (prev_freq, prev_char))
        prev_freq, prev_char = freq + 1, c
    result_str = ''.join(result)
    return result_str if len(result_str) == len(s) else ''

problems.title: Bitwise AND of Numbers Range
problems.description: Given two integers left and right, return the bitwise AND of all numbers in the range [left, right], inclusive.

solutions.python:
def range_bitwise_and(left, right):
    shift = 0
    while left < right:
        left >>= 1
        right >>= 1
        shift += 1
    return left << shift

problems.title: Diagonal Traverse
problems.description: Given an m x n matrix, return all elements in diagonal order, alternating direction each diagonal (the classic zig-zag diagonal scan).

solutions.python:
def find_diagonal_order(mat):
    if not mat:
        return []
    rows, cols = len(mat), len(mat[0])
    result = []
    for d in range(rows + cols - 1):
        indices = []
        r = 0 if d < cols else d - cols + 1
        c = d if d < cols else cols - 1
        while r < rows and c >= 0:
            indices.append(mat[r][c])
            r += 1
            c -= 1
        if d % 2 == 0:
            indices.reverse()
        result.extend(indices)
    return result

problems.description: Given an integer n, return the number of trailing zeroes in n! (n factorial), in logarithmic time.

solutions.python:
def trailing_zeroes(n):
    count = 0
    power_of_five = 5
    while power_of_five <= n:
        count += n // power_of_five
        power_of_five *= 5
    return count

problems.title: Sort List
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list, sort it in ascending order using merge sort, achieving O(n log n) time and O(1) extra space (beyond recursion).

solutions.python:
def sort_list(head):
    if not head or not head.next:
        return head
    slow, fast = head, head.next
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    mid = slow.next
    slow.next = None
    left = sort_list(head)
    right = sort_list(mid)
    dummy = ListNode()
    tail = dummy
    while left and right:
        if left.val <= right.val:
            tail.next = left
            left = left.next
        else:
            tail.next = right
            right = right.next
        tail = tail.next
    tail.next = left if left else right
    return dummy.next

problems.description: Given a list of products and a search word, return a list of up to 3 lexicographically smallest matching products (by prefix) after each character the user types.

solutions.python:
def suggested_products(products, search_word):
    products.sort()
    result = []
    prefix = ''
    for c in search_word:
        prefix += c
        matches = [p for p in products if p.startswith(prefix)][:3]
        result.append(matches)
    return result

problems.title: Design Tic-Tac-Toe
problems.description: Design a Tic-Tac-Toe game played on an n x n board by two players, with a move(row, col, player) function that returns the winning player's id (or 0 if no winner yet), in O(n) time per move.

solutions.python:
class TicTacToe:
    def __init__(self, n):
        self.n = n
        self.rows = [0] * n
        self.cols = [0] * n
        self.diag = 0
        self.anti_diag = 0

    def move(self, row, col, player):
        delta = 1 if player == 1 else -1
        self.rows[row] += delta
        self.cols[col] += delta
        if row == col:
            self.diag += delta
        if row + col == self.n - 1:
            self.anti_diag += delta
        if abs(self.rows[row]) == self.n or abs(self.cols[col]) == self.n or \
           abs(self.diag) == self.n or abs(self.anti_diag) == self.n:
            return player
        return 0
